gallery_guess: 硯展（與石為友 / 文人與硯）
ocr_text:
鳳池硯
蘇軾曾將福建建州的鳳凰山產石（一說為南平的黯黮灘石），作硯至美，命名為鳳味，後人援引蘇軾的說法，將硯面設計為鳳形，稱為鳳池硯。
Inkstone with Phoenix-shaped Pool
Fengchi Inkstone refers to an inkstone design inspired by the imagery of a phoenix. Su Shi (also known as Su Dongpo), a renowned poet and scholar from the Song Dynasty, praised the beauty of a stone from Fenghuang Mountain in Jianzhou, Fujian (alternatively said to be from An Tan Beach in Nanping), and named it "feng zhou." Later, people followed Su Shi's description and designed inkstones in the shape of a phoenix, which came to be known as "feng chi yan", or "Fengchi Inkstone."
caption: 介紹鳳池硯設計典故的告示牌，左緣可見前一塊牌（書經卦象與綬帶紋）殘留文字。

ocr_text:
圭形硯
玉圭，是祭器的禮器，也是諸侯朝覲天子時手中的持物，代表統治地方的權杖，因此圭形硯有出仕為官之意。
Inkstone with Gui Decoration
The "yu gui" has significance as a ritual object used in ceremonies and was also held by feudal lords when they paid visits to the emperor. It symbolized their authority over their territories. The design of an inkstone in the shape of a "yu gui" carries the meaning of pursuing a career in government service or officialdom.
caption: 牆面圖文牌，左側展示一方刻有銘文的灰色圭形硯照片。

artifact_name_en: Inkstone with Gui Decoration / Well-field Inkstone
ocr_text:
圭形硯
玉圭，是祭器的禮器，也是諸侯朝覲天子時手中的持物，代表統治地方的權杖，因此圭形硯有出仕為官之意。
Inkstone with Gui Decoration
The "yu gui" has significance as a ritual object used in ceremonies and was also held by feudal lords when they paid visits to the emperor. It symbolized their authority over their territories. The design of an inkstone in the shape of a "yu gui" carries the meaning of pursuing a career in government service or officialdom.
井田硯
井田是指將田地畫分成井字形的方塊，士人以筆墨為生，將硯稱為「硯田」，以筆耕田，或戲謔自比為耕田的牛。
Well-field Inkstone
Well-field Inkstone refers to an inkstone design that is reminiscent of the layout of land distribution in a checkerboard pattern. Scholars who made their living through writing likened their inkstone to their "tian," meaning fields. They humorously associated, using their pens to "cultivate" their "fields" of knowledge or jokingly comparing themselves to oxen tilling the land. This metaphor highlights the significance of their writing tools in their intellectual endeavors.
caption: 並列兩段文字牌，左為圭形硯、右為井田硯的說明。

ocr_text:
平坦自然的板硯
幾無硯池與硯堂的區別，硯石線性的紋理，被想像像是雨絲；硯石邊皮等處，水侵蝕或風化的凹痕，稱為蟲蛀，是刻意將石材本身的特點以更直接的手法呈現。
Flat and Natural Inkstone
There is little distinction between the ink reservoir and the ink grinding area on this type of inkstone. The linear texture on the surface of the inkstone is often imagined as raindrops. The grooves, eroded by water or weathered by wind, on the edges and surface of the inkstone are called "chong zhu," which translates to "insect holes." This deliberate design feature brings out the stone's inherent characteristics in a more direct manner.
caption: 牆面圖文牌，右側展示一方表面帶銘文的深色板硯照片。

ocr_text:
硯作長方形，硯面的硯堂平坦，硯池呈一字形，中央刻一圓柱形石眼，象徵明月，周圍環繞淺浮雕離的流雲。硯底為斜面，從尾端向前端掏空，尾端無牆，兩側壁有高牆，滿佈圓柱狀的石眼，有如星空。
The inkstone has a rectangular shape. At the center of the reservoir, a cylindrical stone eye is carved, symbolizing the bright moon, while delicate shallow-relief cloud motifs surround it. The bottom of the inkstone slopes, gradually hollowing out from the rear to the front, resembling a starry sky.
caption: 昏暗展櫃內一方深色長方硯與旁邊的玻璃壓克力說明牌。
notes: 拍攝偏暗、反光，文字部分模糊但可辨。

ocr_text:
觸摸體驗區
Touch Experience Area
3D列印
傳宋 從星硯 蘇軾銘
3D Reproduction
Inkstone with astrological theme
Engraved with poem by Su Shi
Attributed to Song dynasty (960-1127)
硯背 The bottom of the inkstone
caption: 觸摸體驗區大型告示牌，下方照片為從星硯硯背佈滿圓柱狀石眼。
觸摸體驗區的3D列印從星硯複製品，翻轉展示硯背密佈圓柱狀石眼。

gallery_guess: 硯展（愛硯成痴的米芾與蘇軾）
ocr_text:
愛硯成痴的米芾與蘇軾
Two Inkstone Aficionados — Mi Fu and Su Shi
蘇軾和米芾是兩個好朋友，蘇軾曾借走米芾珍愛的紫金硯，臨終時囑咐兒子以硯陪葬，最後米芾把紫金硯取回，激動又欣喜的寫下這段軼事，正是聞名的書跡〈紫金研帖〉。
蘇軾好硯，喜愛藏硯，各種名硯無不盡藏，又常撰寫硯銘，加上後代文人對蘇軾的仰慕，遂使蘇軾像及其平生韻事，如〈赤壁賦〉，都成為硯石常見的題材。
Su Shi and Mi Fu were close friends. Su Shi once borrowed Mi Fu's cherished purple-gold inkstone and, on his deathbed, instructed his son to bury him with the inkstone. Eventually, Mi Fu retrieved the purple-gold inkstone and, filled with excitement and joy, wrote down this incident. This well-known anecdote is captured in the famous calligraphic work "Zi-jin-yan-tie," which recounts their deep affection for inkstones.
Su Shi had a deep affection for inkstones and was an avid collector of them. He possessed a wide variety of renowned inkstones and often composed inscriptions for them. The admiration of later generations for Su Shi further solidified his influence, leading to the incorporation of his poetic legacy into the world of inkstones. His famous works like the "Red Cliffs Ode" became common themes depicted on inkstones.
宋 米芾 紫金研帖 A Note for the Purple and Gold Inkstone, Mi Fu, Song dynasty
傳宋 蘇軾銘 從星硯 硯側 Side of inkstone with astrological theme and engraved with poem by Su Shi, Attributed to Song dynasty (960-1127)
明至清 十七世紀 端石 背面刻蘇軾像 Bottom of Duan inkstone, carved with figure of Su Shi, 17th century, Ming to Qing dynasty
caption: 牆面大段介紹米芾與蘇軾愛硯軼事，右側三張相關書跡與硯石照片。

ocr_text:
硯石材質緻密溫潤，石色棕黑，橫紋間佈，如夜空浮雲；石眼點點，黃綠而微紅，彷彿星空棋佈，正呼應右側壁所刻的蘇軾硯銘：「月之從星，時則風雨，汪洋翰墨，將此是似，墨雲浮空，漫不見天，風起雲移，星月凜然。」
The inkstone material is dense and warm, with a deep brown-black hue. The spread of horizontal lines resembles floating clouds in a night sky. The stone eyes, small spots on the stone, are a mix of yellow-green with hints of red, resembling stars scattered across the sky. This imagery resonates with the inscription on the right-side wall, penned by Su Shi himself.
caption: 展櫃全景，後方牆面為從星硯放大照片與硯銘，前方壓克力座上放置從星硯實物及立架。

ocr_text:
硯石材質緻密溫潤，石色棕黑，橫紋間佈，如夜空浮雲；石眼點點，黃綠而微紅，彷彿星空棋佈，正呼應右側壁所刻的蘇軾硯銘：「月之從星，時則風雨，汪洋翰墨，將此是似，墨雲浮空，漫不見天，風起雲移，星月凜然。」
The inkstone material is dense and warm, with a deep brown-black hue. The spread of horizontal lines resembles floating clouds in a night sky. The stone eyes, small spots on the stone, are a mix of yellow-green with hints of red, resembling stars scattered across the sky. This imagery resonates with the inscription on the right-side wall, penned by Su Shi himself.
caption: 從星硯放大照片牆與硯銘，下方壓克力座放置實物硯及立架。

artifact_name_en: Inkstone with astrological theme / Duan inkstone
dynasty: 傳宋（960-1127）／明至清 十七世紀（17th century, Ming to Qing dynasty）
ocr_text:
傳宋
從星硯
蘇軾銘
Inkstone with astrological theme
Engraved with poem by Su Shi
Attributed to Song dynasty (960-1127)
贈文 000152
明至清 十七世紀
端石硯
背刻蘇軾像
Duan inkstone
Back carved with figure of Su Shi
17th century, Ming to Qing dynasty
贈文 000018
caption: 展櫃內並放兩張名稱牌：從星硯與背刻蘇軾像的端石硯。
notes: 編號依「贈文」後數字判讀，端石硯編號末位略糊。

ocr_text:
隨形硯
Inkstone with Natural Form
常稱為「子石」，指大石中的精石，多為自然圓石，石理瑩潤，保留石材原形，略加修整，古樸天成。
Usually referred to as "Zi Shi," which means the finest stone among larger stones, this type of inkstone is typically a naturally rounded stone. The stone surface is smooth and glossy, preserving the original shape of the stone with minimal modifications, resulting in an archaic and naturally beautiful appearance.
caption: 直式告示牌，介紹隨形硯（子石）。

ocr_text:
子石硯
宋人米芾愛硯也愛石，是著眼於天然石本身的詭譎瑰麗，而明代對奇石的愛好又多了仙山、隱居的意涵。畫中庭園的疊石假山，四面環水，猶如孤島，與世隔離。
Zishi Inkstone
Zishi Inkstone refers to an inkstone design that imitates the appearance of piled rocks in a garden or rockery. During the Song Dynasty, Mi Fu, a renowned scholar and artist, had a deep appreciation for both inkstones and natural rocks. He focused on the enchanting and intricate beauty of natural stones. In the Ming Dynasty, the love for peculiar rocks took on connotations of fairy mountains and seclusion. Paintings often depicted rockeries in gardens, surrounded by water on all sides, resembling isolated islands that are separated from the rest of the world.
Peaks of Gathered ...
聚石
caption: 牆面圖文牌，右側為山水畫局部，繪有題「聚石」的奇石。
notes: 畫作英文標題右緣被裁切。

ocr_text:
海天硯
Sea and Sky Inkstone
以海天為主題，有仙界樓閣或雲龍穿梭，硯體包含硯側均滿飾浮雕，硯堂、墨池彷彿置身於廣大無垠的波濤或雲間。
Inspired by the sea and the sky, this type of inkstone features celestial realms with heavenly pavilions or dragons soaring through the clouds. The body of the inkstone is adorned with intricate relief carvings on all sides, while the inkwell and ink reservoir seem to be immersed in vast and boundless waves or clouds.
caption: 直式告示牌，介紹海天硯。

ocr_text:
海天硯
一望無垠的海域足以寄託士人夢想中的桃花源，萬頃波濤之間亦易於引發乘龍來去的謬想，硯有形而其氣勢卻收放隨意，無限開展。
Haitian Inkstone
Haitian Inkstone refers to an inkstone design that captures the vast expanse of the sea and sky. This design serves as a vessel for scholars to project their dreams of an idyllic utopia, much like the legendary "Peach Blossom Spring." The endless waves and sea horizon easily evoke fanciful thoughts of riding dragons to traverse the vast waters. While the inkstone has a defined physical form, its grandeur and energy seem to expand and contract with an unbounded sense of freedom and openness.
caption: 牆面圖文牌，右側展示一方滿佈浮雕與銘文的海天硯照片。

ocr_text:
肖形硯
Figurative Inkstone
以樹幹、葉、瓜、竹等為造型，硯面及硯背均一體考量，為一完整的立體雕刻品，硯堂等部份亦融入其間，設計極富巧思。
The figurative inkstone takes the form of tree trunks, leaves, gourds, bamboo, and other elements. The design considers both the surface and the back at the same time, creating a complete three-dimensional sculpture. The ink reservoir and other components of the inkstone are seamlessly integrated, showcasing intricate and ingenious details.
caption: 直式告示牌，介紹肖形硯。
展台上陳列數件硯：左為木座嵌綠色硯，後立架鑲魚化石板，前方兩件綠色玉質魚式／葉式硯。

ocr_text:
魚式硯
水中悠遊的魚兒，具有「如魚得水」的美好寓意，清代取自東北龍興之地的魚化石又另有一層飲水思源、永不忘祖的警惕之意。
Fish-shaped Inkstone
Fish-shaped Inkstone refers to an inkstone design inspired by the graceful movements of fish swimming in water. The imagery of fish in water carries the beautiful connotation of being in one's element, which means feeling comfortable and at ease. During the Qing Dynasty, the design of fish-shaped inkstones drew from fossilized fish found in the homeland of Manchurian. This design also carries the metaphorical significance of "drinking water and remembering its source," serving as a reminder to never forget one's roots and heritage.
caption: 牆面文字牌，左為魚式硯說明，右緣為下一段蕉葉枯木與竹造形硯文字。

artifact_name_zh: 蕉葉、枯木與竹的造形硯
artifact_name_en: Inkstones in shapes of Banana Leaf, Withered Wood and Bamboo
ocr_text:
蕉葉、枯木與竹的造形硯
「雨打芭蕉」與「蕉葉題詩」是文學的浪漫，枯木老幹是擷取自大自然的強勁生命力，竹子則具有許多美好的意涵。竹筍可作佳餚，竹林清雅怡人，而像徵君子德性的竹節，更成為製硯發揮的重點，無論縱觀或橫視之，均可賞、可用。
Inkstones in shapes of Banana Leaf, Withered Wood and Bamboo
"Raindrops on Banana Leaves" and "Inscribing Poems on Banana Leaves" are romantic literary expressions. "Withered Wood" draws from the strength of nature's resilience, portraying the tenacity of life even in its mature stage. "Bamboo" symbolizes lots of goodness. Bamboo shoots are enjoyed as delicacies, bamboo groves create a tranquil atmosphere. The symbolism of the Bamboo nodes, representing the virtue of a noble person, becomes a key element in crafting inkstones. Regardless of the perspective, these designs are both visually appealing and functional.
caption: 牆面文字牌，主體為蕉葉枯木竹造形硯說明，左緣可見前一段魚式硯殘字。

ocr_text:
與硯為友
Befriending the Inkstones
硯原是文人賴以為生的工具，文人與硯建立良好的友誼之後，洗硯、製硯盒，以愛護、照顧他們；為硯傳形拓模，撰寫硯譜，留下記錄；還有愛硯人一起賞硯、談硯，形成硯交圈。您會為自己珍愛的收藏品做些什麼？
The inkstone was originally a vital tool for literati, and after establishing a strong bond with their inkstones, scholars would wash them, craft inkstone boxes, and care for them with love and attention. They would create rubbings and write inkstone catalogues to document their inkstones. Apart from this, scholars would gather with fellow inkstone enthusiasts to appreciate and talk about inkstones, forming a community of inkstone experts. What would you do for your cherished collections?
caption: 展區大段引言牆面，標題「與硯為友」，左側為大型直書「與硯為友」字樣。

gallery_guess: 硯展（與硯為友 / 文化記憶）
ocr_text:
文化記憶
Cultural Memory
硯石堅固，猶如碑石，可歷代相傳，形成文化記憶的載體。硯上文化名人的刻銘，引發今月曾經照古人之嘆，而以蘭亭修禊與羲之換鵝旳故事為題材，或是表現蘇軾雋永的文學傳奇，更是硯石最愛傳誦的內涵。
Inkstones, with their durable nature akin to stone tablets, can be passed down through generations and become carriers of cultural memory. The engraved inscriptions of cultural figures on inkstones evoke admiration and reflection on the past. Themes such as the Orchid Pavilion Gathering and Writing Scriptures for Geese serve as inspirations while portraying the timeless literary legends of Su Shi.
caption: 「文化記憶」展區引言牆面，背景為淡墨雲山。

ocr_text:
硯的珍藏
Preserving the Inkstone
硯不可一日不洗滌，洗硯除了去垢，還要以清水養石潤。為了避免燥石，收藏硯的硯盒不宜用金屬材質，大多為漆盒或木質，量身訂製。再者，凡經手、過目的佳硯，沒有相機也無妨，繪圖、模拓，加上文字描述，留下永恆的記錄。
An inkstone should never go unwashed for a day. Cleaning the inkstone with clear water not only removes impurities but also nourishes the stone. To prevent the inkstone from drying out, it is advisable to store it in a dedicated inkstone box made of materials such as lacquer or wood rather than metal. These boxes are often custom-made to fit the inkstone precisely. Furthermore, for cherished inkstones that have been appreciated and examined, much have been passed down even without a camera. Drawing, making rubbings, and adding written descriptions can create enduring records, preserving their beauty and significance for an eternity.
caption: 「硯的珍藏」展區引言牆面。

gallery_guess: 硯展（硯的珍藏 / 硯譜）
ocr_text:
硯譜——黑白寫真集
高濂將所見的〈奇研圖〉二十方，以墨繪硯形，輔以文字，記錄尺寸、石色及刻銘。清高宗敕撰《欽定西清硯譜》以內府所藏硯，由內廷畫家門應兆負責以西洋光影法描繪，最為精美。高鳳翰的《硯史》則為一私人藏硯愛硯者的嘔心瀝血心聲之作。
Inkstone Catalog—A Monochrome Photobook
Gao Lian meticulously created the "Qi Yan Tu", a collection of twenty inkstone illustrations, using ink to depict the forms of various inkstones. Accompanied by text, he recorded their dimensions, colors, and inscriptions. During the Qing Dynasty, Emperor Qianlong had the inkstones from the imperial collection captured using Western lighting techniques by court painter Men Yingzhao, resulting in exquisite renditions. Gao Fenghan's "Yan Shi", on the other hand, is a heartfelt work from a private inkstone enthusiast. It encapsulates the passion and dedication of those who cherish and collect inkstones.
caption: 牆面圖文牌，介紹硯譜，右側為古籍木刻硯圖局部。

artifact_name_zh: 奇研圖（古瓦觷研／碧玉圭研）
artifact_name_en: Illustration of Curious Inkstone, from Eight Treatises on Following the Principles of Life
ocr_text:
古瓦觷研
碧玉圭研
雅尚齋遵生八牋，奇研圖，明高濂撰，明刊本
Illustration of Curious Inkstone, from Eight Treatises on Following the Principles of Life, Gao Lian, Imprint of Ming dynasty (1368-1644)
caption: 古籍《遵生八牋‧奇研圖》跨頁木刻插圖，左頁古瓦觷研、右頁碧玉圭研。
notes: 硯名「古瓦觷研」「碧玉圭研」依木刻篆隸標題判讀，可能有出入。

ocr_text:
洗硯
硯用畢應以水浸軟，去垢而不傷硯；洗滌宿墨可用蓮房殼：池邊斑駁的墨跡稱為墨繡，不可磨去，是古硯的表徵。
Washing Inkstones
After using the inkstone, it is best to soften it by soaking it in water to remove dirt without damaging the stone. For cleaning old ink residue, lotus seedpod powder can be used. The blotchy ink marks near the inkstone's water reservoir are known as "mo xiu", and they shouldn't be polished away, as they are emblematic of the antique inkstone.
caption: 直式告示牌，介紹洗硯方法。

gallery_guess: 硯展（硯的珍藏 / 硯盒）
material: 木、漆、犀皮、螺鈿、銀絲、嵌美石
ocr_text:
硯盒——定製一個舒適的窩
以木、漆等為材質的硯盒，盒蓋以犀皮、螺鈿、銀絲或嵌美石的技法為飾，又或表現龍紋或仙人等吉祥母題，是為愛硯訂做一個安全、輕巧又優雅的住所。
Inkstone Box - Customizing a Comfortable Nest
Inkstone boxes crafted from materials like wood and lacquer are adorned with decorative elements such as xipi lacquer, mother-of-pearl inlay, silver wire, or exquisite gemstones. These decorations often portray auspicious themes like dragon patterns or immortal figures. These boxes provide a secure, lightweight, and elegant home tailored for cherished inkstones.
caption: 牆面圖文牌，右側展示一件犀皮漆紋硯盒蓋面照片。

artifact_name_zh: 嵌玉硯盒及木匣（內盛蕉葉白硯）
artifact_name_en: Inkstone box with jade inlay and wooden case
caption: 展台陳列一組硯具：刻銘漆木盒蓋、深色硯台、木匣與嵌玉盒蓋並列。
notes: 實物無文字牌入鏡，名稱見IMG_4858。硯台刻銘文字過小未能逐字辨識。

artifact_name_en: Inkstone box with jade inlay and wooden case
dynasty: 清 十九世紀（19th century, Qing dynasty）
ocr_text:
清 十九世紀
嵌玉硯盒及木匣
內盛蕉葉白硯
Inkstone box with jade inlay and wooden case
With banana-white inkstone
19th century, Qing dynasty
Donated by Ms. Xu Lu Hanbo
徐陸寒波女士捐贈
贈文 000027
caption: 嵌玉硯盒及木匣名稱牌，標示清十九世紀、徐陸寒波女士捐贈。
